title: A King's Ransom Creates a Currency
story: Captured by the English during the Hundred Years War, King John II of France needed a hefty ransom. To raise the funds, a brand new currency was born in 1360. title: Soil-Free Farming Takes Root
story: Plants growing without soil? It's not magic, it's hydroponics! On December 5, 1935, the first commercial hydroponics farm opened in Montebello, California. title: New York City Fights Housing Discrimination
story: Imagine finding your dream home, only to be turned away because of your race or religion. In 1957, New York City said 'No more!' to this unfair practice. On December 5, the city passed a groundbreaking law called the Fair Housing Practices Law. It made it illegal for landlords and sellers to discriminate against people based on their race or faith when renting or selling homes.
